arts.ttl: Thibaut Courtois Responds to Simeone’s Comments After Atletico’s Champions League Exit
arts.sum: Simeone had made headlines in the post-match press conference when he aggressively questioned journalists about Julián Álvarez’s double touch during his penalty attempt. The Belgian goalkeeper, speaking in the mixed zone after the game, dismissed Simeone’s complaints about the disallowed penalty and criticized the Atletico manager’s reaction.

arts.ttl: Diego Simeone’s Post-Game Reaction to Atletico’s Champions League Exit Against Real Madrid
arts.sum: Atletico Madrid ’s Champions League campaign came to an agonizing and cruel end for them at the Metropolitano, as they fell to Real Madrid in a dramatic penalty shootout in the Round of 16. Despite Simeone’s challenge, video evidence clearly confirmed that Álvarez had indeed touched the ball twice, leading to the disallowance of the penalty in accordance with the rules.

arts.ttl: Player Ratings: Atletico Madrid 1-0 Real Madrid (2-2 aet, 2-4 penalties) 2025 UCL Round of 16
arts.sum: Produced one of the defensive plays of the season with a 30 yard run and slide tackle as the last man back to deny Atleti a clear counter towards goal. Fede Valverde—8.5: In the final stages of the match when every other player was depleted, Fede looked like he could play another 90 minutes.

arts.ttl: The inevitability of Real Madrid breaking Atléti hearts in the UCL
arts.sum: Real Madrid and Atlético couldn't be separated over two legs but, when Wednesday's match went to penalties, there was only ever going to be one outcome.

arts.ttl: Champions League quarterfinal predictions, from Real Madrid-Arsenal to Villa-PSG
arts.athr: Gab Marcotti and Mark Ogden and Julien Laurens and Rob Dawson and Beth Lindop
arts.sum: So, the round of 16 is over and after some amazing Champions League action, what will happen in the next round? ESPN FC's experts give their knee-jerk picks for the quarterfinals.

arts.ttl: Ancelotti: "A penalty shootout is a lottery and it went our way"
arts.sum: Carlo Ancelotti appeared in the Metropolitano press room to assess Real Madrid's qualification for the Champions League quarter-finals following victory over Atlético on penalties: "It was a difficult game, and even more so when you concede inside a minute. They're well drilled, defend and work hard for each other, and we had a great chance through that incredible Mbappé move but we missed the penalty and it was level until the shootout.

arts.ttl: Thibaut Courtois ‘sensed’ Julian Alvarez ‘double touch’ before VAR controversy
arts.sum: The penalty was disallowed for a rarely seen ‘double touch’ as Real Madrid reached the quarter-finals

arts.ttl: Real Madrid beat Atletico Madrid in Champions League last-16 after penalty drama
arts.sum: The holders are through after a dramatic penalty shootout

arts.ttl: Raheem Sterling out of Real Madrid showdown as Declan Rice and Arsenal star Champions League suspension risks
arts.sum: Another key Gunners player is now a booking away from an automatic one-match ban, with yellow card not wiped until after the quarter-finals

arts.ttl: Ancelotti: “I still think penalty shootouts are a lottery”
arts.sum: Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti was asked what it takes to win a penalty shootout after his side beat Atlético Madrid to eliminate them from the UEFA Champions League . The priority was to look for effective possession and opportunities, but without complicating the game.
